mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during periods above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate signal aggressive monetary tightening to combat price pressures, which rapidly compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise concurrently, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and aggregate demand, directly boosting corporate earnings expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict positive returns, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail auto sales exceeding 5 percent signals a sharp contraction in consumer discretionary spending, which immediately pressures equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive returns in >55% of triggered months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly with t-test p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently low unemployment and rising housing starts indicate a synchronized labor and housing market expansion, which amplifies consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal shows no predictive edge over individual series, or forward returns are negative in >60% of co-occurring observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust economic expansion, which validates corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above the threshold coincides with negative quarterly returns in >50% of cases, or the signal loses statistical significance during high-inflation environments.
